bird.name: 棕尾鵟
bird.summary: 棕尾鵟（学名：Buteo rufinus）为鹰科鵟属的鸟类。多栖息于开阔的平原和草原。该物种的模式产地在苏丹。 保护 中国国家重点保护动物等级：二级 亚种 棕尾鵟指名亚种（学名：Buteo rufinus rufinus）。在中国大陆，分布于新疆、甘肃、云南、西藏等地。

locations.name: 岸
locations.summary: 岸（又稱濱），分為海岸、湖岸及河岸，是在水面和陆地接触处，经波浪、潮汐、海流等作用下形成的滨水地带，其中有眾多沉積物堆積而形成的岸稱為灘。 海水作用 波浪，經常造成侵蝕 海流，經常造成堆積 潮汐 海岸類型 依構成物質區分 可分為岩岸與沙岸…

locations.id: loc-熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林-dbaa79b4
locations.name: 熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林
locations.summary: 热带及亚热带旱生阔叶林（英語：tropical and subtropical dry broadleaf forests），简称旱生林（dry forest）是一种WWF定义的栖地生境并用来命名以此为代表的生物群系。其所需的气候类型较常…

relations.subject: 棕尾鵟
relations.evidence: thumb|A long-legged buzzard perching in a Picea orientalis|spruce in Turkey. The long-legged buzzard inhabits open, uncultivated areas, with high bushes, trees, cliffs or hillocks are favoured as nesting areas as well as access to freshwater. The species normally lives in steppe, semi-desert and desert edge, barren rocky landscapes, dry shrubland and sometimes sea coasts

relations.subject: 棕尾鵟
relations.object_id: loc-熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林-dbaa79b4
relations.object: 熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林
relations.evidence: 3(1–2): 67–72. While long-legged buzzards predominantly forage in wildlands, they are also adaptable to cultivations, pastures, village outskirts and sometimes even heavily farmed areas. Grasslands are widely used during winter. Those wintering in the Indian subcontinent largely use similar arid open plains, semi-deserts and cultivated areas but perhaps surprisingly are considered a characteristic wintering raptor of dry mixed forests with open glades and barren hill slopes. In the Indian subcontinent, the species may often be seen using a variety of perches including bushes, hedges, Acacia nilotica, sand dunes, haystacks, mounds and power poles. The North African and Arabian race may reportedly show a strong preference for open wooded and/or rocky areas but has been reported in a similarly broad amount of habitats as the nominate subspecies. The species may occur from sea-level up to about in Europe, but in Asia uncommonly lives in mountains at altitudes of up to or even with migrants recorded to . Younger birds disperse north of breeding grounds and there are records from Northern Europe
